message:
perf(fcp-raptorq): reduce per-pivot heap allocation in Gaussian elimination and simplify dense fallback

Gaussian elimination inner loop: refactored sparse_update_columns_if_beneficial
into sparse_update_columns_into that accepts a caller-provided Vec buffer,
reused across all pivots in the elimination phase. This eliminates one
Vec allocation per pivot row in the sparse update path. The small-row
fast path now uses a cleaner two-pass count-then-collect pattern, and
the large-row path uses cols.len() instead of a separate counter.

Dense fallback reconstruction: simplified try_reconstruct_dense to build
the constraint matrix directly from self.received rather than going
through the intermediate symbols Vec. Missing source identity rows are
replaced with repair LT equations from the received repair symbols.
Removed the post-decode cross-verification loop from the primary decode
path — the InactivationDecoder's verify_decoded_output is sufficient for
well-determined systems. Removed the CorruptDecodedOutput fallback branch
since the dense solver is now only used via explicit try_reconstruct_dense
calls.

- decoder.rs: sparse_update_columns_into with reusable buffer, reusable
  sparse_col_buf in InactivationDecoder::solve
- decode.rs: simplified dense fallback, removed redundant verification loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
